task: I’m looking at two used cars this weekend and want to keep my notes organized while I’m at the lot. Can you help me think through a straightforward way to compare the main facts so I don’t miss anything important while I’m checking them out?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — the easiest way is to use the same checklist for both cars and fill it out side by side while you’re there.

## Simple comparison template

| Category | Car 1 | Car 2 |
|---|---|---|
| Year / Make / Model / Trim |  |  |
| Mileage |  |  |
| Asking price |  |  |
| VIN |  |  |
| Number of owners |  |  |
| Accident history |  |  |
| Title status |  |  |
| Service records available? |  |  |
| Interior condition |  |  |
| Exterior condition |  |  |
| Tires / brakes condition |  |  |
| Warning lights on? |  |  |
| Test drive notes |  |  |
| Features you care about |  |  |
| Dealer / seller notes |  |  |
| Your overall score / impression |  |  |

## What to focus on at the lot
If you want to keep it simple, make sure you capture these first:

1. **Basic identity**  
   Year, trim, mileage, VIN, price.

2. **History**
   Accident report, title status, number of owners, service history.

3. **Condition**
   Tires, brakes, body damage, interior wear, leaks, warning lights.

4. **Drive feel**
   Acceleration, braking, steering, noise, vibration, transmission smoothness.

5. **Must-have features**
   Backup camera, AWD, heated seats, CarPlay, etc.

## Quick rating idea
You can give each car a score from 1–5 in a few areas:
- Price/value
- Condition
- History
- Test drive
- Features

Then add them up for a rough comparison.

## Handy notes format
If you prefer plain notes, use this for each car:

- **Pros:**  
- **Cons:**  
- **Questions to ask:**  
- **Red flags:**  
- **Would I buy it? Why/why not?**

If you want, I can also turn this into a **one-page printable checklist** or a **phone-friendly note template** you can copy into your Notes app.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that works. But I’d want it a little more field-useful, not just a generic list.

For me, I’d rather have a quick compare sheet with the stuff that actually changes the decision:
- price
- mileage
- title history
- service records
- tire age / wear
- brake condition
- any warning lights
- cold start behavior
- test drive notes
- rust / underbody condition
- any signs of flood or body repair

And I’d want a separate spot for ‘red flags’ because that’s usually what matters most once you’re standing there looking at both cars.

If you can, make it into something I can just copy into my phone notes with checkboxes or short lines. That’s easier than a table I have to keep scrolling through at the lot.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a **phone-notes-friendly compare sheet** you can copy/paste and fill in fast at the lot.

---

If you want, I can also turn this into an even **shorter “one-screen” version** for faster note-taking while you’re walking around the lot.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s usable. The phone-notes format is better — I can actually see myself filling that in without fighting the screen.

The red flags section separate like that is the part I wanted. And putting flood/body repair signs in its own spot matters, because that’s the stuff I don’t want buried.

So yeah, this works.
